title: DDL, Constraints, Schema Evolution, and Integrity Repair · Independent Query Trial
summary: The support schema must reject impossible states and evolve while old application versions are still active. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a constraint-rich, reversible schema migration with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define tables with deliberate types, defaults, generated values, and named constraints.
Enforce NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, primary-key, and foreign-key invariants at the database boundary.
Choose restrict, cascade, set-null, or set-default actions from lifecycle ownership semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.title: Evidence defense
